claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm asking about my daughter Alex – she's 6 years old and lives with me here in the UK. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know that you have Alex, who is 6 years old and lives with you in the UK.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for her needs such as food, clothes, and accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm her parent and I take care of all her needs – I provide her food, buy her clothes, pay for where we live, all of that. She lives with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming. 

Just to make sure I have all the information I need: Is there anyone else who is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else claimed it for her?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her. It's just me looking after her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16), which meets the age requirement
- She lives with you full-time in the UK
- You are responsible for her care and provide for all her needs (food, clothes, accommodation)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for Alex.

Please note: If you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - it just means you might need to pay some or all of it back through tax.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, so she meets the age requirement. She lives with you in the UK full-time, and you are responsible for her care - providing food, clothes, and accommodation. You are her parent and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for her. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is the parent of one child, Alex (age 6), who lives with them full-time in the UK. The claimant provides all care and financial support for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

She lives with you in the UK full-time, and you are responsible for her care - providing food, clothes, and accommodation. You are her parent and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for her. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

The claimant provides all care and financial support for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
